title: Critical/High PSIRT Alerting

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracks Cisco PSIRT security advisories affecting managed devices, providing evidence of vulnerability awareness and remediation tracking as required by NIST 800-53 Flaw Remediation.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates continuous monitoring of vendor-published security advisories for all managed network infrastructure.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search over index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory scheduled daily; advisory counts and affected device lists exported to compliance dashboard.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Contributes to vulnerability scanning by tracking known vendor-published vulnerabilities, though it does not replace active scanning tools.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides a vendor-authoritative vulnerability feed for network infrastructure to complement active vulnerability scanning.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard panel showing advisory severity distribution and affected device counts, refreshed with each TA poll cycle.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Alerts on critical and high severity vendor security advisories, supporting PCI DSS requirements to identify and rank vulnerabilities.
compliance.controlObjective: Ensures critical vendor-published vulnerabilities are flagged within hours of detection by Catalyst Center.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Alert action on UC-5.13.35 firing for severity CRITICAL/HIGH advisories; alert history in triggered alerts log.

dataSources: index=catalyst, sourcetype cisco:dnac:securityadvisory (severity, deviceId, cveId, advisoryId, advisoryTitle)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" (severity="CRITICAL" OR severity="HIGH") | stats dc(deviceId) as affected_devices values(cveId) as cves latest(advisoryId) as advisory by severity, advisoryTitle | sort severity -affected_devices
description: Alerts on critical and high severity PSIRTs affecting managed devices, with the count of affected devices and associated CVEs.
value: Critical and high PSIRTs require immediate remediation. Alerting with affected device counts helps prioritize patching based on blast radius.
implementation: Enable the `securityadvisory` input. Use as a scheduled alert with low latency for CRITICAL, and include advisoryTitle and cves in the payload for analysts.
visualization: Table (affected_devices, cves, advisory by severity and title), top-N list for P1 remediation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: index=catalyst, sourcetype cisco:dnac:securityadvisory (severity, deviceId, cveId, advisoryId, advisoryTitle).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable the `securityadvisory` input. Use as a scheduled alert with low latency for CRITICAL, and include advisoryTitle and cves in the payload for analysts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" (severity="CRITICAL" OR severity="HIGH") | stats dc(deviceId) as affected_devices values(cveId) as cves latest(advisoryId) as advisory by severity, advisoryTitle | sort severity -affected_devices
```

Understanding this SPL

**Critical/High PSIRT Alerting** — Critical and high PSIRTs require immediate remediation. Alerting with affected device counts helps prioritize patching based on blast radius.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Filters the advisory stream down to the two most severe `severity` levels.
• `stats` measures distinct impacted `deviceId` values, collects related `cveId` values, and keeps the most recent `advisoryId` per `severity` and `advisoryTitle` group.
• `sort` orders by `severity` then descending `affected_devices` to emphasise the largest exposures.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions as required. Consider visualizations: Table (affected_devices, cves, advisory by severity and title), top-N list for P1 remediation.
grandmaExplanation: We immediately flag the most dangerous security vulnerabilities found on your network devices and tell you how many devices are at risk.
